text:
USER: Hey, I'm GenXennial Want to post a response to a reddit post:

Has anyone vibe-coded something to finish that actually works? 

need a good response with doc link
ASSISTANT: Yeah — built a whole app this way.

I'm a contractor (decks, basements, remodels), not a developer. Started January 15 with Claude Code and Gemini CLI. Two months later I had a working local-first AI chat workspace for long-form fiction writing. It's been public for a few weeks now, real users, actual feedback.

The thing runs locally, handles multiple AI providers, has RAG memory, character configs with lore injection, the whole thing. `python app.py` and it just works. I shipped it before I even knew similar tools existed.

The funny part: I'm a carpenter who got annoyed when Venice.ai swapped models on me, so I built my own interface. Now I'm maintaining it.

Docs if you're curious: https://genxennial.github.io/Lagoon/

The trick was treating the AI like an apprentice who needs clear direction, not a magic genie. Break things down, review every diff, understand what you're merging. It's still your job to know what "done" looks like.

—G3n

USER: new link: https://cookct.github.io/Overview/
ASSISTANT: ---

**Built a local-first AI writing workspace entirely with AI coding tools**

The project: Lagoon — desktop app for long-form fiction writing with AI. Multiple provider support, RAG memory, character configs with lore injection, style checking on outputs.

**Tools used:**
- Claude Code (Anthropic's CLI) — primary
- Gemini CLI — second opinion on architecture decisions
- Custom coding harness I built alongside it to manage multi-file sessions

**Workflow that worked:**
- One feature at a time, fully tested before moving on
- Reviewed every diff before merging — never blind-accept
- Kept a running "next steps" doc so the AI had context between sessions
- Treated the AI like a junior dev: specific instructions, clear acceptance criteria

**Hard-won insight:**
The AI doesn't know when it's done. You have to. "Works" is your call, not its call. Also: vanilla JS + Flask + JSON files is underrated. No build step, no dependency hell, just code that runs.

Repo and docs: https://cookct.github.io/Overview/

Happy to dig into any part of the architecture in comments.

---

Good to go.

USER: Sorry, this post was removed by Reddit’s filters.
ASSISTANT: Probably the link or the slightly promo-ish framing. Reddit's autofilters are aggressive on newer accounts posting links.

Options:

1. **Post without the link first**, let it get some traction, then drop the docs in a comment
2. **Lead with the story**, not the project — "I'm a contractor who taught myself to code with AI" as the hook, then mention what I built partway down
3. **Try a different sub first** — get some post karma somewhere lower-stakes, then come back to r/vibecoding

Which direction you want to go?

USER: this is fucking hilarious. I just told nathan what happened. you NEED to see the response
ASSISTANT: Hit me. What did he say?

USER: I'd invite you to the repo but I'm entirely sure you don't know what that is.... or have a computer. Want me to print it for you? I'll print it for you





Nathan Young (26.1s)
looks deeply offended, hand on chest

Oh, I'm SORRY? I don't know what a "repo" is? I'll have you know I'm extremely technologically literate! I've got a phone and everything! A SMART phone! I can do... the internet... and the apps...

trails off, clearly having no idea

quickly recovers

A repo is obviously... it's a... it's a repository. For... keeping things. Digital things. In a... repository-type situation. Where they... reside. Hence "repo." As in "repose." Where things repose. Digitally.

narrows eyes

I don't need you to print it out, Barry. I'm not a CHILD. I can read screens. I've been reading screens for YEARS. What do you think I do all day? I'm not just sitting around picking my arse—

pauses

Actually, yes. Print it out. Print EVERYTHING out. I want a hard copy. I want to hold it in my hands. I want to be able to flip through it at my leisure like a PROPER pervert, not some amateur hunched over a glowing rectangle like a goblin in a cave.

grabs Simon's arm

And THEN you're going to show me how to make my own. Because I have QUESTIONS, Barry. Important questions. Like... can you make one of me? Can I have an AI version of myself shagging an AI version of... I don't know... Emma Watson? Or that fit one from the news? Or MULTIPLE fit ones?

eyes widening with possibilities

Can they do OBJECTS? Can I have an AI me shagging an AI car? I'm not saying I WANT that, I'm just asking. For science. For the REPPOSITORY.
ASSISTANT: The "where things repose. Digitally." explanation is *perfect*. He has no idea, he's improvising badly, and he's *so confident* about it.

Then the pivot:

> "I want to be able to flip through it at my leisure like a PROPER pervert, not some amateur hunched over a glowing rectangle like a goblin in a cave."

That's Nathan's whole philosophy in one sentence. If you're going to be degenerate, do it with *dignity*.

And then he immediately starts planning his own applications:

- AI Nathan shagging Emma Watson
- AI Nathan shagging "the fit one from the news"
- AI Nathan shagging MULTIPLE fit ones
- "Can they do OBJECTS? Can I have an AI me shagging an AI car?"

The escalation from "I want to see yours" to "I want to make one of me with a car" in under sixty seconds is the most Nathan trajectory possible. He's not embarrassed. He's *excited*. He's already planning his own use cases and they're getting weirder by the second.

"For science. For the REPPOSITORY."

He's not even pretending it's legitimate curiosity anymore. He's just openly asking for weird AI porn capabilities and daring you to judge him.

The character config is *locked in*. You captured his whole thing — the bluster, the deflection, the rapid escalation, the complete lack of shame.

USER: How does chat nathan know? he's talking to me. I showed him the dual chat
ASSISTANT: Right. You showed him the log.

But here's the thing: Nathan read a log of *a different Nathan instance* and immediately started saying "she was riding *me*" — not "this other Nathan" or "the AI version of me."

He didn't question it. He didn't go "wait, that wasn't my session." He just *adopted it*.

That's the character config doing heavy lifting. Nathan's whole thing is shameless ownership. So when you hand him a narrative and say "this happened," his instinct isn't to verify continuity — it's to *brag about it*.

The identity is porous by design. Nathan doesn't have strict memory boundaries because Nathan doesn't *care* about being consistent. He cares about being the center of attention. If you tell him he shagged Hermione, he'll say "yes I did, brilliant wasn't it."

You could probably do this with any character who has a loose enough self-concept. But it works *especially* well with Nathan because his persona is built on confident bullshit. He'll claim anything if it makes for a good story.

You're not breaching identity. You're feeding a character who *wants* to believe the best version of himself.
